# Current capability matrix

As of **2026-07-13**, generated offline from [`protocols/capability_catalog_v1.json`](../protocols/capability_catalog_v1.json) (SHA-256 `7a063d882b5661df442e4dd9d10fd38de705773c5c70b5a32589f4a96a10d38b`). Regenerate with `python scripts/generate_capability_matrix.py --write`.

This is a software/evidence inventory, not a scientific result. `tested` means behavior on the named local proof path only; it does not itself assign E3 relationship evidence or imply scientific conformance, deployment security, estimator application validity, or EC1/H1–H4 success. The current matrix contains **0** `validated` row(s).

Status and evidence level are orthogonal. Status semantics are fail-closed: `implemented` = code/dependency without a named proof command; `tested` = named local proof; `validated` = E4/E5 end-to-end or independent evidence; `specified` = design/interface only; `deferred` = E0 unavailable/off-path, including rejected records, and is never a delivery promise. The E0–E5 column follows grandplan §8.9 strictly: local-only tested features stay E0, E2 requires an immutable external dependency, and E3 requires a pinned producer and consumer tested together against golden fixtures. Exact local revisions are content hashes, avoiding a self-referential future Git commit hash. The generator checks schema, canonical pins, paths, and hashes; catalog review and CI execution—not static command-text inspection—establish that a named proof actually exercises its declared inputs.

| Feature | Status | Exact revision | Test command | Evidence artifact | Known limitations | Evidence level | Thesis dependency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Report-first PID estimate eligibility and abstention | <code>tested</code> | <code>pid-rs@ac4a7803c5a77408f5e9176c60cda71c65c38260; local-sha256:05c6e259572a608a6c0e6c2b40cc671d20621935140da2c3f573821a42250aec</code> | <code>just estimate-report-contract</code> | [`crates/pid-sim/fixtures/offline_vlda_fixture.json`](../crates/pid-sim/fixtures/offline_vlda_fixture.json)<br>[`crates/pid-sim/fixtures/offline_vlda_continuous_fixture.json`](../crates/pid-sim/fixtures/offline_vlda_continuous_fixture.json)<br>[`crates/pid-sim/src/offline_harness.rs`](../crates/pid-sim/src/offline_harness.rs)<br>[`findings.md`](../findings.md) | The high-dimensional MI/coherence route is NO-GO and continuous shared-exclusions atoms on real VLA embeddings remain application-blocked; an abstention is not a zero estimate. | **E3** (build tested adapter) — pinned pid-rs producer and Prisoma consumer tested together on positive and abstaining fixtures | conditional<br>claims: H3 |
| Agent Bridge typed control plane | <code>tested</code> | <code>local-sha256:d2d3d5732a23cbb926200206ad79bd495d46a4a801c2ee96872f2c45a6733253</code> | <code>just bridge-security</code> | [`crates/pid-bridge`](../crates/pid-bridge)<br>[`crates/pid-rerun/src/runlog.rs`](../crates/pid-rerun/src/runlog.rs)<br>[`crates/pid-sim/src/lib.rs`](../crates/pid-sim/src/lib.rs)<br>[`crates/pid-sim/src/bin/bridge_stdio.rs`](../crates/pid-sim/src/bin/bridge_stdio.rs)<br>[`crates/pid-sim/src/bin/bridge_tcp.rs`](../crates/pid-sim/src/bin/bridge_tcp.rs)<br>[`crates/pid-sim/src/bin/bridge_ws.rs`](../crates/pid-sim/src/bin/bridge_ws.rs)<br>[`ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md) | TCP/WebSocket binaries refuse non-loopback bind addresses and default safe, but forwarding/proxying/tunnelling a loopback listener is not prevented. TCP/stdio JSONL lines have a 1 MiB cap, WebSocket upgrade/incoming-frame inputs have 16 KiB/1 MiB caps, and network read/write operations have 30-second timeouts; there is no total request/session, request-count, or aggregate-traffic limit, so progress-making trickle traffic may persist. The enumerated WebSocket gate requires GET /bridge HTTP/1.1, exactly one each of a nonempty Host, Upgrade websocket, tokenized Connection containing upgrade, version 13, and a base64 16-byte key, and no Origin; it does not claim general malformed-request detection. Client application messages are unfragmented, masked UTF-8 text frames; ping, pong, and close control frames are supported, while binary frames, fragmentation, and extensions/RSV use are rejected. The JSON-RPC 2.0 surface is single-request only: missing-id notifications are silent and distinct from explicit null, parameters are omitted or named objects rather than positional arrays, undeclared top-level method keys are rejected, sim.step requires numeric dt, and batches are unsupported. Profile-invalid parameters use -32602; handler/domain failures after validation use -32000. File RPCs provide non-adversarial canonical confinement and no-replace outputs, not a security-grade sandbox against hardlinks, aliases, or concurrent filesystem mutation. Export parses/manifests the same exact byte snapshot read from the source and stages/syncs/installs no-clobber finalized RRD bytes/hash. Executable transport run logs call File::sync_all for the initial prefix, each session flush before a wire response, and the terminal seal; generic SimBridgeSession<W> durability is sink-defined. There is no parent-directory fsync, power-loss durability, or cross-file run-log/export transaction. Crashes or provenance-storage failures may leave incomplete/unreadable logs, an apparently complete terminal record with indeterminate status/durability, or orphan RRDs. There is no authentication, authorization, TLS, redaction, remote-security assessment, full target UI/VLA/backend control coverage, or versioned subscription stream; actor identity remains locally declared. | **E0** (intention or local only) — local unit-tested single-request dispatch, bind/default/wire caps, enumerated WebSocket checks, and non-adversarial canonical/no-replace file behavior; not remote-security, forwarding/proxy, adversarial-filesystem, crash-durability, or scientific validation | required<br>claims: EC1 |
